---
title: "Nonlinear MPC"
---

## What "Nonlinear" Means

Nonlinear MPC (NMPC) directly optimizes with nonlinear dynamics and possibly nonlinear constraints.

General form:

$$
x_{k+1} = f(x_k, u_k)
$$

with trajectory optimization over a finite horizon.

## Why Use NMPC

Use NMPC when linear approximations are not sufficient, for example:

- aggressive maneuvers,
- strong nonlinear vehicle/robot dynamics,
- geometry-heavy constraints.

## Trade-Offs

- Better fidelity and potentially better performance.
- Higher compute burden and solver sensitivity.
- More challenging warm starts and real-time guarantees.

## Practical Engineering Considerations

- Good initial guesses are critical.
- Real-time iteration strategies are common.
- Constraint softening and regularization improve robustness.

Back to main MPC page: [MPC Overview](mpc.qmd).
